title: Alnylam — White Space
html:
Module 7: Alnylam White-Space BD Map

Last Updated: April 30, 2026 Purpose: Identify, prioritize, and action-plan every unsold ZoomRx service opportunity across Alnylam's brand portfolio. Built for use in BD conversations, AO planning, and pipeline reviews.

Section 1: Full Brand x Service Matrix

How to Read This Matrix

  • Active -- currently running or recently delivered
  • HIGH -- strong fit, natural extension, client likely receptive
  • MEDIUM -- good fit but needs introduction, pilot, or champion building
  • LOW -- possible but not a natural fit right now
  • N/A -- service does not fit this brand/TA

ATTR Amyloidosis: AMVUTTRA (vutrisiran) & ONPATTRO (patisiran)

Service AMVUTTRA (US) AMVUTTRA (International) ONPATTRO
PET / SFEA Active (quarterly, 4 manufacturers) Active (5 countries, semi-annual) Active (France PET only)
ATU Active (ATTR-CM quarterly, hATTR-PN one wave) MEDIUM -- country-level ATU would complement international PET N/A (declining brand)
PCA (Patient Chart Audit) Active (semi-annual, 300+100 charts) HIGH -- international PCA would capture country-level treatment patterns and combination therapy N/A
Integrated Analysis Active (PET+ATU+PCA, ADOPT funnel) MEDIUM -- extend ADOPT framework to international data N/A
Message Recall (Patient) Active (Patient Message Recall study) MEDIUM -- patient message recall in key international markets N/A
MSL / MEE HIGH -- ATTR-CM launch created significant MSL engagement with cardiologists; no MSL effectiveness tracking currently in place MEDIUM -- MSL engagement varies by country; could pilot in 1-2 markets N/A
Digital / TV Tracking Active (TV Tracking 2024) LOW -- digital promotional landscape varies too much by country N/A
HCP-Patient Conversations MEDIUM -- ATTR-CM treatment decision involves complex shared decision-making (injection vs. oral, mechanism education) LOW N/A
Patient Experience HIGH -- ATTR patients on quarterly SC injection with vitamin A supplementation; combination therapy patients face dual treatment burden; patient journey from diagnosis through treatment is complex LOW N/A
Message Testing HIGH -- ATTR-CM is a new indication; rapid TTR knockdown message is under-delivered; message refresh needed given Q4 2024 effectiveness decline MEDIUM -- country-specific message testing for trailing markets (Japan, Italy) N/A
Qual Research MEDIUM -- qual on combination therapy decisions, cardiologist vs. neurologist prescribing rationale LOW N/A
Commercial Analytics (SFTO/Rx Integration) MEDIUM -- integrate PET with prescribing claims to quantify rep impact on Rx behavior (proven at J&J with ERLEADA/DARZALEX SFTO) LOW N/A
Sales Simulation LOW N/A N/A
Launch KPI Tracking HIGH -- ATTR-CM launched March 2025; launch KPI tracking for the cardiology audience would measure adoption velocity in a new specialty N/A N/A
Social Listening MEDIUM -- ATTR patient communities and HELIOS-B data discussion online LOW N/A
Ferma Congress MEDIUM -- ATTR data presented at major cardiology congresses (AHA, ACC, ESC) LOW N/A
Patient Scribe MEDIUM -- AI-powered capture of ATTR patient diagnostic and treatment journey narratives N/A N/A

Acute Hepatic Porphyria: GIVLAARI (givosiran)

Service GIVLAARI
PET / SFEA Active (US, semi-annual) -- but STALE (last data Q2 2023). HIGH priority for PET REFRESH
ATU Active (Global: US, Germany, Brazil; annual)
PCA MEDIUM -- AHP patient charts would track treatment patterns (prophylactic vs. as-needed hemin vs. GIVLAARI) and Type 4/5 expansion
Integrated Analysis HIGH -- PET+ATU integration currently missing. ATU W4 disease perception shift not reflected in PET. Integration would bridge this gap
MSL / MEE MEDIUM -- AHP MSLs interact with porphyria specialists and referring physicians; tracking their impact on diagnosis and treatment decisions could be valuable
Digital Tracking LOW -- ultra-rare; limited digital promotional presence
HCP-Patient Conversations MEDIUM -- AHP treatment decisions involve complex discussions about chronic management, injection burden, and attack threshold triggers
Patient Experience MEDIUM -- monthly SC injection for a lifelong condition; chronic symptoms between attacks; patient QoL data would support the chronicity narrative
Diagnostic Journey Study HIGH -- this study type does not currently exist in the Alnylam portfolio. The 15-year average diagnostic delay is the #1 growth bottleneck. A study tracking the patient path from symptom onset to AHP diagnosis (referral patterns, misdiagnosis history, testing behavior) would address the fundamental commercial challenge. ZoomRx could pioneer this as a new study type
Message Testing MEDIUM -- message refresh needed to test chronicity-focused messaging (chronic symptom improvement vs. attack prevention framing)
Qual Research MEDIUM -- qual on the Converter segment (22% of HCPs, high patient load, low GIVLAARI share) to understand why they question GIVLAARI's superiority over hemin
Brazil Access / Payer Study MEDIUM -- dedicated study addressing Brazil's structural access challenges (~45% of diagnosed patients not on consistent treatment). Different methodology from the ATU
Ferma Congress LOW
Social Listening LOW -- ultra-rare; minimal online patient community

Primary Hyperoxaluria Type 1: OXLUMO (lumasiran)

Service OXLUMO
PET / SFEA Active (head-to-head vs Rivfloza; semi-annual)
ATU Active (Wave 1 Dec 2024; 60 HCPs)
PCA MEDIUM -- patient chart audit tracking siRNA switching patterns (OXLUMO to Rivfloza and vice versa), combination use (siRNA + B6), and transplant deferral outcomes
Integrated Analysis MEDIUM -- PET+ATU integration for PH1, similar to ATTR ADOPT model
MSL / MEE MEDIUM -- MSL impact data shows MSLs impact understanding of appropriate use; dedicated MSL assessment could be valuable (similar to Genmab Epkinly model)
Urologist Education Module HIGH -- urologists are significantly less familiar with PH1 treatment options, less likely to suspect PH1, and less likely to order genetic testing. A dedicated urologist module testing genetic testing adoption, PH1 suspicion triggers, and referral patterns fills a major gap. This could be a standalone study or an ATU add-on
Patient/Caregiver Experience MEDIUM -- lifelong therapy; administration convenience debate (HCP-administered quarterly vs self-administered monthly); no patient/caregiver voice currently in any study. Given self-administration being the top competitive message, patient preference data is strategically important
Message Testing MEDIUM -- test messaging variations around the all-stages label advantage (OX2) which has high effectiveness but low recall
Switching/Persistence Study MEDIUM -- as both OXLUMO and Rivfloza are established, tracking dynamic switching behavior (OXLUMO to Rivfloza, Rivfloza to OXLUMO, B6 to siRNA) provides retention intelligence
Digital Tracking LOW -- ultra-rare; limited digital landscape
Qual Research MEDIUM -- qual with nephrologists on the access perception gap (why do they perceive Rivfloza as more affordable without experience?)
Social Listening LOW
Ferma Congress LOW

Section 2: Top 10 White-Space Opportunities (Ranked)

Rank 1: AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM MSL / MEE Tracking

Dimension Detail
Brand AMVUTTRA (ATTR-CM)
Service MSL / MEE (Medical Engagement Effectiveness)
Estimated Revenue $150-250K/year (US MSL effectiveness study; international pilot adds $50-100K per market)
Why it ranks #1 ATTR-CM approval (March 2025) triggered a massive expansion of Alnylam's cardiology MSL team. These MSLs are engaging cardiologists -- the exact audience where AMVUTTRA has a reach gap. Yet there is zero MSL effectiveness tracking in place. The international PET data shows the cardiologist reach problem is universal; MSL engagement may be a key lever to close the gap. ZoomRx already runs MEE at J&J across all TAs -- the methodology is proven and can be adapted for Alnylam
Entry Strategy Lead with the cardiologist reach gap data: "Your PET shows AMVUTTRA reps are not reaching enough cardiologists. Your MSLs are -- but you have no visibility into whether those MSL interactions are translating into clinical understanding and prescribing intent. MEE fills that gap." Reference the J&J MEE rollout as proof of enterprise-scale methodology
Who to Pitch [TTR insights leadership -- populate from contacts.md]; escalate to field medical leadership
Timeline Propose Q3 2026; field Q4 2026

Rank 2: GIVLAARI PET Refresh

Dimension Detail
Brand GIVLAARI (AHP)
Service PET (Refreshed)
Estimated Revenue $80-120K per wave (2 waves/year = $160-240K/year)
Why it ranks #2 The last GIVLAARI PET data is from Q2 2023 -- over 3 years old. During that gap, ATU Wave 4 (Oct 2024) captured a fundamental shift: HCPs now view AHP as a chronic condition, and the top GIVLAARI treatment goal shifted from attack prevention to chronic symptom improvement. The current PET does not measure whether reps have adapted their messaging to this new reality. The Converter segment (22%, high patient load, low GIVLAARI share) needs specific messaging data that only a refreshed PET can provide
Entry Strategy "Your ATU shows AHP perception has fundamentally shifted since your last PET. Are your reps leading with chronic symptom messages or still leading with attack prevention? A refreshed PET answers that question and validates whether your field force has caught up with physician thinking."
Who to Pitch Elizabeth Bergantino (Market Insights -- GIVLAARI contact)
Timeline Propose immediately; this is a data gap that should concern Alnylam

Rank 3: AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM Launch KPI Tracking

Dimension Detail
Brand AMVUTTRA (ATTR-CM indication)
Service Launch KPI Tracking
Estimated Revenue $120-180K (time-bound, 6-12 months)
Why it ranks #3 AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM approval (March 2025) was a new-indication launch into a new specialty (cardiology). Over a year in, there is no dedicated launch dashboard measuring cardiology-specific adoption velocity, cardiologist awareness trajectory, or ATTR-CM vs. hATTR-PN prescribing dynamics. The PET and ATU capture elements of this but are not structured as launch KPI tracking. A dedicated launch dashboard -- designed from the cardiologist's perspective -- would give Alnylam real-time adoption metrics for the most important launch in the company's history
Entry Strategy "You have the most important indication launch in Alnylam's history and no dedicated launch dashboard for cardiology. Your existing studies track promotional effectiveness and awareness, but they were designed before the ATTR-CM launch. A cardiology-specific launch KPI tracker measures what matters: adoption velocity among cardiologists, time-to-trial, source-of-share from tafamidis/acoramidis."
Who to Pitch [TTR insights leadership]; [ATTR-CM commercial lead]
Timeline Propose Q2 2026; can field rapidly using existing PET infrastructure

Rank 4: AMVUTTRA Message Testing (ATTR-CM)

Dimension Detail
Brand AMVUTTRA
Service Message Testing
Estimated Revenue $80-120K per round (2 rounds likely = $160-240K)
Why it ranks #4 Q4 2024 US PET showed message effectiveness declining 10-31 points across nearly ALL AMVUTTRA messages. The rapid TTR knockdown message -- the most motivating message in every market -- is recalled in only 33-50% of details. Meanwhile, safety messages are over-delivered and under-performing. This data clearly shows the current message portfolio needs refreshing. Message testing would allow Alnylam to: (1) develop cardiology-specific messages, (2) test updated efficacy narratives leveraging longer-term HELIOS-B data, and (3) address the message fatigue signal
Entry Strategy "Your PET shows message effectiveness declined 10-31 points in Q4. Your most motivating message (rapid knockdown) is recalled in only 33-50% of details while your least effective message (safety) is most recalled. Message testing develops the next-generation message portfolio and tests cardiology-specific framing."
Who to Pitch [AMVUTTRA brand insights lead]; reference the safety trap pattern across all international markets
Timeline Propose Q2 2026; field Q3 2026

Rank 5: AHP Diagnostic Journey Study

Dimension Detail
Brand GIVLAARI (AHP)
Service Diagnostic Journey Study (new study type)
Estimated Revenue $100-150K (one-time or annual refresh)
Why it ranks #5 The 15-year average diagnostic delay is the #1 commercial bottleneck for GIVLAARI. Every newly diagnosed patient is a potential GIVLAARI patient because there are no real competitors. Yet no ZoomRx study currently measures the patient journey from symptom onset to AHP diagnosis. This is the most productive strategic lever available: compressing diagnostic delay directly expands the addressable market. A diagnostic journey study tracking referral patterns (59% of US patients referred from PCPs/GI), misdiagnosis rates (26%), testing behavior, and time-to-diagnosis would be the first-of-its-kind for this account
Entry Strategy "Your ATU shows 59% of AHP patients are referred from other specialties, the average diagnostic delay is 15 years, and 26% are initially misdiagnosed. But you don't know WHERE in the journey patients are lost or which referring physician behaviors to target. A diagnostic journey study maps the patient path and identifies the highest-ROI intervention points."
Who to Pitch Elizabeth Bergantino; escalate to rare disease commercial leadership
Timeline Propose Q3 2026

Rank 6: OXLUMO Urologist Education Module

Dimension Detail
Brand OXLUMO (PH1)
Service Dedicated Urologist Module (ATU add-on or standalone)
Estimated Revenue $60-100K (standalone) or $30-50K (ATU add-on)
Why it ranks #6 ATU Wave 1 confirmed that urologists are significantly less familiar with PH1 treatments beyond supportive care and transplant. They are less likely to suspect PH1, less likely to order genetic testing, and focus on stone management rather than metabolic disease treatment. Yet urologists see the majority of kidney stone patients -- they are the front line for PH1 identification. A dedicated urologist module testing genetic testing adoption, PH1 suspicion triggers, and referral patterns fills a gap that the current mixed-specialty ATU cannot adequately address
Entry Strategy "Your ATU shows urologists are the key untapped audience for PH1 awareness. But your current studies mix nephrology and urology analysis with low urology sample sizes. A dedicated urologist module goes deep on the exact questions that matter: when do they suspect PH1, what triggers genetic testing, and where do referral pathways break?"
Who to Pitch Elizabeth Bergantino; [OXLUMO brand lead]
Timeline Propose alongside ATU Wave 2 planning

Rank 7: AMVUTTRA Patient Experience Tracking

Dimension Detail
Brand AMVUTTRA
Service Patient Experience Tracking
Estimated Revenue $120-180K/year
Why it ranks #7 ATTR-CM patients face a complex treatment experience: quarterly SC injections, vitamin A supplementation, potential combination therapy (silencer + stabilizer), disease progression monitoring, and multidisciplinary care across cardiology and neurology. As combination therapy becomes more common (15% of PCA CM charts already), patient burden increases. No patient-facing study exists in the Alnylam portfolio. Patient experience data would support Strategic Imperative #3 (optimize customer experience, minimize non-clinical barriers)
Entry Strategy "Your PCA shows 15% of ATTR-CM patients are already on combination therapy. That means dual treatment burden, dual access navigation, and dual side effect management. But you have zero patient-level experience data. Patient experience tracking captures what PCA cannot -- the lived experience of being on AMVUTTRA."
Who to Pitch [TTR insights leadership]; [Market access team -- access barriers are the #3 strategic imperative]
Timeline Q3-Q4 2026

Rank 8: International PCA (ATTR)

Dimension Detail
Brand AMVUTTRA (International)
Service Patient Chart Audit (International)
Estimated Revenue $80-120K per country (2-3 priority countries = $160-360K)
Why it ranks #8 The US PCA captures treatment patterns and combination therapy, but the international PET has no equivalent patient-level data. As ATTRUBY/BEYONTTRA launches internationally (Bayer in EU, Alexion in Japan, 2026-2027), understanding country-level treatment patterns becomes critical. Priority markets: Spain (strongest AMVUTTRA market -- validate with prescribing data) and Japan (weakest -- understand barriers at patient level)
Entry Strategy "You know AMVUTTRA's SOV in 5 countries. But SOV does not tell you what physicians actually prescribe. An international PCA gives you the prescribing-level data to match your promotional data -- and prepares you for ATTRUBY's international launch when the competitive landscape gets more complex."
Who to Pitch [International commercial leads]; [International PET stakeholders]
Timeline Align with ATTRUBY international launch timeline (2026-2027)

Rank 9: GIVLAARI PET+ATU Integration

Dimension Detail
Brand GIVLAARI (AHP)
Service Integrated Analysis (PET + ATU)
Estimated Revenue $40-60K (integration deliverable layered on existing studies)
Why it ranks #9 The ATTR program has an Integrated Analysis (PET+ATU+PCA into ADOPT). GIVLAARI does not -- the PET and ATU exist independently, creating blind spots. The ATU captures disease beliefs and treatment patterns; the PET captures promotional effectiveness. But there is no bridge connecting what reps say to how physicians actually behave. The ATU segmentation (Enthusiasts/Trialists/Converters/Inexperienced) should inform which segments reps target and what messages they lead with -- but this is not reflected in PET design
Entry Strategy "Your ATTR program has integrated analysis. Your GIVLAARI program does not. The disease perception shift you captured in ATU Wave 4 should be shaping how your reps detail GIVLAARI -- but without integration, you cannot tell if it is."
Who to Pitch Elizabeth Bergantino
Timeline Propose alongside GIVLAARI PET refresh (Rank #2)

Rank 10: OXLUMO Patient/Caregiver Preference Study

Dimension Detail
Brand OXLUMO (PH1)
Service Patient/Caregiver Preference Study
Estimated Revenue $80-120K (one-time)
Why it ranks #10 All current PH1 studies are HCP-facing. There is zero patient/caregiver data on administration preference, treatment burden, quality of life, or switching drivers. Given that Rivfloza's self-administration message (RI9) is the single most effective message across BOTH brands (ME up to 90%), understanding whether patients actually prefer self-administration monthly vs. HCP-administered quarterly is strategically critical. If patients prefer OXLUMO's quarterly schedule despite it being HCP-administered, Alnylam has a powerful counter-narrative
Entry Strategy "Rivfloza's most effective message is self-administration. But you don't know what patients actually want -- quarterly visits vs. monthly self-injection for a lifelong therapy. Patient preference data either validates the Rivfloza threat or gives you the counter-narrative."
Who to Pitch Elizabeth Bergantino; [OXLUMO commercial lead]
Timeline Q4 2026

Revenue Summary: Top 10 Opportunities

Rank Opportunity Est. Revenue Probability Weighted
1 AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM MSL/MEE $150-250K 50% $100K
2 GIVLAARI PET Refresh $160-240K 65% $130K
3 AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM Launch KPI $120-180K 45% $68K
4 AMVUTTRA Message Testing $160-240K 50% $100K
5 AHP Diagnostic Journey Study $100-150K 40% $50K
6 OXLUMO Urologist Module $60-100K 55% $44K
7 AMVUTTRA Patient Experience $120-180K 35% $53K
8 International PCA (ATTR) $160-360K 30% $78K
9 GIVLAARI PET+ATU Integration $40-60K 60% $30K
10 OXLUMO Patient/Caregiver Study $80-120K 35% $35K
TOTAL $1.15-1.88M $688K

Section 3: Competitive Gap Analysis

Where Competitors Could Enter and ZoomRx Is Not Yet Defending

Competitive Scenario ZoomRx Vulnerability Defensive Action
Cardiology-native research vendor captures ATTR-CM tracking As Alnylam expands into cardiology, they may seek vendors with established cardiology panels. ZoomRx's rare disease heritage could be perceived as a neurological specialty bias Demonstrate cardiology-specific analysis in every ATTR deliverable. Proactively propose MEE for cardiology MSLs (Rank #1). Show that ZoomRx's panel includes cardiologists at scale
Large international vendor (IQVIA/Ipsos/Kantar) captures international PET These vendors have broader multi-country infrastructure and may offer lower per-market fielding costs ZoomRx's moat is the cross-study integration -- international PET data connected to US PET + ATU + PCA in the ADOPT framework. No international-only vendor can replicate this. Ensure every international deliverable connects back to US data
Novo Nordisk commissions competing PH1 research If Novo Nordisk funds its own PH1 tracking studies, Rivfloza-focused data could enter the market and challenge ZoomRx's findings Maintain head-to-head objectivity in the OXLUMO PET. ZoomRx's value is blinded competitive intelligence from a neutral party -- protect this positioning
Alnylam internal analytics grows As Alnylam scales toward $10B, internal market research capabilities may develop Position ZoomRx's proprietary HCP panel, blinded competitive benchmarking, and cross-manufacturer tracking as irreplaceable external primary data that internal teams cannot generate

Section 4: Quick-Reference Action Calendar

When Action Target Contact
May 2026 Propose GIVLAARI PET Refresh with chronicity narrative framing Elizabeth Bergantino
May 2026 Propose AMVUTTRA Message Testing citing Q4 2024 effectiveness decline [TTR insights lead]
June 2026 Propose AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM MSL/MEE using cardiologist reach gap data [TTR insights lead]; [Field medical leadership]
June 2026 Propose AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM Launch KPI for cardiology audience [ATTR-CM commercial lead]
July 2026 Propose AHP Diagnostic Journey Study for GIVLAARI growth acceleration Elizabeth Bergantino; rare disease commercial lead
July 2026 Propose OXLUMO Urologist Module alongside ATU Wave 2 planning Elizabeth Bergantino
Q3 2026 Propose AMVUTTRA Patient Experience Tracking [TTR insights leadership]
Q3 2026 Propose GIVLAARI PET+ATU Integration deliverable Elizabeth Bergantino
Q4 2026 Propose International PCA (Spain + Japan as pilot markets) [International commercial leads]
Q4 2026 Propose OXLUMO Patient/Caregiver Preference Study Elizabeth Bergantino
Ongoing Monitor CARDIO-TTRansform data readout (mid-2026) -- triggers Wainua PET restart and potential study redesign All contacts

This map should be refreshed quarterly as projects close, new data emerges, and the competitive landscape shifts. The CARDIO-TTRansform readout (mid-2026) and ATTRUBY international launches (2026-2027) are the two upcoming events most likely to reshape the white-space picture. Next scheduled refresh: July 2026.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

Rank 1: AMVUTTRA ATTR-CM MSL / MEE Tracking

Dimension

The last GIVLAARI PET data is from Q2 2023 -- over 3 years old. During that gap, ATU Wave 4 (Oct 2024) captured a fundamental shift: HCPs now view AHP as a chronic condition, and the top GIVLAARI treatment goal shifted from attack prevention to chronic symptom improvement. The current PET does not measure whether reps have adapted their messaging to this new reality. The Converter segment (22%, high patient load, low GIVLAARI share) needs specific messaging data that only a refreshed PET can provide


Entry Strategy
"Your ATU shows AHP perception has fundamentally shifted since your last PET. Are your reps leading with chronic symptom messages or still leading with attack prevention? A refreshed PET answers that question and validates whether your field force has caught up with physician thinking."


Who to Pitch
Elizabeth Bergantino (Market Insights -- GIVLAARI contact)


Timeline
Propose immediately; this is a data gap that should concern Alnylam

Rank 5: AHP Diagnostic Journey Study



Dimension
Detail




Brand
GIVLAARI (AHP)


Service

Estimated Revenue

Why it ranks #5
The 15-year average diagnostic delay is the #1 commercial bottleneck for GIVLAARI. Every newly diagnosed patient is a potential GIVLAARI patient because there are no real competitors. Yet no ZoomRx study currently measures the patient journey from symptom onset to AHP diagnosis. This is the most productive strategic lever available: compressing diagnostic delay directly expands the addressable market. A diagnostic journey study tracking referral patterns (59% of US patients referred from PCPs/GI), misdiagnosis rates (26%), testing behavior, and time-to-diagnosis would be the first-of-its-kind for this account


Entry Strategy
"Your ATU shows 59% of AHP patients are referred from other specialties, the average diagnostic delay is 15 years, and 26% are initially misdiagnosed. But you don't know WHERE in the journey patients are lost or which referring physician behaviors to target. A diagnostic journey study maps the patient path and identifies the highest-ROI intervention points."


Who to Pitch
Elizabeth Bergantino; escalate to rare disease commercial leadership


Timeline
Propose Q3 2026

Dimension
Detail




Brand

Estimated Revenue

Why it ranks #6
ATU Wave 1 confirmed that urologists are significantly less familiar with PH1 treatments beyond supportive care and transplant. They are less likely to suspect PH1, less likely to order genetic testing, and focus on stone management rather than metabolic disease treatment. Yet urologists see the majority of kidney stone patients -- they are the front line for PH1 identification. A dedicated urologist module testing genetic testing adoption, PH1 suspicion triggers, and referral patterns fills a gap that the current mixed-specialty ATU cannot adequately address

Section 3: Competitive Gap Analysis
Where Competitors Could Enter and ZoomRx Is Not Yet Defending



Competitive Scenario
ZoomRx Vulnerability
Defensive Action




Cardiology-native research vendor captures ATTR-CM tracking
As Alnylam expands into cardiology, they may seek vendors with established cardiology panels. ZoomRx's rare disease heritage could be perceived as a neurological specialty bias
Demonstrate cardiology-specific analysis in every ATTR deliverable. Proactively propose MEE for cardiology MSLs (Rank #1). Show that ZoomRx's panel includes cardiologists at scale


Large international vendor (IQVIA/Ipsos/Kantar) captures international PET
These vendors have broader multi-country infrastructure and may offer lower per-market fielding costs
ZoomRx's moat is the cross-study integration -- international PET data connected to US PET + ATU + PCA in the ADOPT framework. No international-only vendor can replicate this. Ensure every international deliverable connects back to US data


Novo Nordisk commissions competing PH1 research
If Novo Nordisk funds its own PH1 tracking studies, Rivfloza-focused data could enter the market and challenge ZoomRx's findings
Maintain head-to-head objectivity in the OXLUMO PET. ZoomRx's value is blinded competitive intelligence from a neutral party -- protect this positioning


Alnylam internal analytics grows
As Alnylam scales toward $10B, internal market research capabilities may develop
Position ZoomRx's proprietary HCP panel, blinded competitive benchmarking, and cross-manufacturer tracking as irreplaceable external primary data that internal teams cannot generate
